rationale: This structure categorizes the field by distinct methodological areas and major application domains rather than philosophical divides like Bayesian vs. Frequentist. This ensures a paper on a 'Bayesian method for causal inference' is classified under 'Causal Inference,' its primary contribution area. We deliberately split the large domain of health statistics into 'Biostatistics,' 'Clinical Trials Methodology,' and 'Statistical Genetics' to reflect distinct, large professional communities with their own journals and conferences. The 'Statistical Learning & Data Mining' category is carefully defined to cover the statistical theory and inferential aspects of machine learning methods, distinguishing it from the more algorithm- and performance-focused work in the 'Computer Science & AI' level-1 category.

subspecialties.name: Biostatistics
subspecialties.tagline: Statistical methods for public health, medicine, and biological research.
explicitly_excludes: Methods for genomics (→ Statistical Genetics)
Clinical trial design (→ Clinical Trials Methodology)
Time-to-event models (→ Survival & Reliability Analysis)

subspecialties.name: Statistical Learning & Data Mining
subspecialties.tagline: Inference, uncertainty, and theory for predictive modeling and algorithms.
explicitly_excludes: Novel algorithm architecture or performance benchmarks (→ Computer Science & AI)

subspecialties.name: Causal Inference
subspecialties.tagline: Methods for estimating cause-and-effect from experimental and observational data.
explicitly_excludes: General experimental design (→ Experimental Design & A/B Testing)

subspecialties.name: Computational Statistics & Simulation
subspecialties.tagline: Algorithms for fitting models and conducting inference. MCMC, VI, bootstrap.
explicitly_excludes: Purely numerical analysis or high-performance computing (→ Computer Science & AI)

subspecialties.name: Clinical Trials Methodology
subspecialties.tagline: Design, conduct, and analysis of clinical trials and medical studies.
explicitly_excludes: General principles of experimentation (→ Experimental Design & A/B Testing)
General methods for health data (→ Biostatistics)

subspecialties.name: High-Dimensional & Nonparametric Statistics
subspecialties.tagline: Methods for complex data, p>>n problems, and flexible modeling.
explicitly_excludes: Foundational probability theory (→ Statistical Theory & Foundations)

subspecialties.name: Statistical Genetics & Bioinformatics
subspecialties.tagline: Statistical methods for genomics, proteomics, and systems biology data.
explicitly_excludes: General biostatistical methods (→ Biostatistics)
Purely biological or computational biology findings (→ Biology)

subspecialties.name: Statistical Theory & Foundations
subspecialties.tagline: The mathematical underpinnings of statistics. Asymptotics, probability, decision theory.
explicitly_excludes: Development of specific Bayesian theory (→ Bayesian Theory & Methods)
Theory for high-dimensional models (→ High-Dimensional & Nonparametric Statistics)

subspecialties.name: Bayesian Theory & Methods
subspecialties.tagline: Development of Bayesian models, priors, and foundational concepts.
explicitly_excludes: Algorithms for Bayesian computation (→ Computational Statistics & Simulation)
Application of Bayesian methods to a specific problem (→ the relevant problem area)

subspecialties.name: Time Series & Sequential Analysis
subspecialties.tagline: Modeling and forecasting data collected over time.
explicitly_excludes: Spatio-temporal models (→ Spatial & Spatio-temporal Statistics)
Econometric applications (→ Economics)

subspecialties.name: Experimental Design & A/B Testing
subspecialties.tagline: Principles for designing informative experiments in science and industry.
explicitly_excludes: Methods specific to clinical trials (→ Clinical Trials Methodology)
Causal inference from observational data (→ Causal Inference)

subspecialties.name: Survival & Reliability Analysis
subspecialties.tagline: Modeling time-to-event data in medicine, engineering, and social sciences.
explicitly_excludes: General longitudinal data methods (→ Biostatistics)

subspecialties.name: Spatial & Spatio-temporal Statistics
subspecialties.tagline: Statistical methods for data with geographic or spatial structure.
explicitly_excludes: Analysis of data over time only (→ Time Series & Sequential Analysis)

subspecialties.name: Survey & Sampling Methods
subspecialties.tagline: Design and analysis of surveys and population sampling techniques.
explicitly_excludes: General experimental design (→ Experimental Design & A/B Testing)

uncovered_concerns: The boundary between 'Statistical Learning' and 'Machine Learning' (in CS & AI) is inherently fuzzy; we rely on author/journal intent to distinguish between statistical properties vs. algorithmic performance.
Fields like Econometrics, Psychometrics, and Actuarial Science are excluded as they are considered domain applications, belonging in Economics, Psychology, and Finance respectively.
Survival Analysis could have been merged into Biostatistics, but was kept separate to also serve its large audience in engineering (reliability) and social sciences.
